fixes_applied.title: SettingsContext.Provider value memoized with useMemo
fixes_applied.description: Added useMemo import and wrapped the Provider value object in useMemo with [settings, loaded, updateSetting] as dependencies. updateSetting is already stable (useCallback with [] deps), so the memoized value only changes when settings or loaded actually change. This prevents all consumers of useGeneralSettings() from re-rendering on every SettingsProvider render.
fixes_applied.lines_changed: import line (added useMemo), lines 255-258 (added useMemo wrapper), line 261 (use memoized value)

fixes_applied.title: Today.tsx — replaced 3 inline new Date() calls with useToday() hook
fixes_applied.description: Replaced `toDateKey(new Date())` with `useToday()` hook which returns a memoized { today, todayDate } that auto-refreshes at midnight. Removed toDateKey import. Replaced `new Date(today + 'T00:00:00')` in TaskInput defaultDueDate with the memoized todayDate from the hook. The remaining `new Date().getHours()` for eat-the-frog morning check is intentionally kept — it needs the actual current hour, not midnight.
fixes_applied.lines_changed: import lines (removed toDateKey, added useToday), line 59 (useToday destructure), line 128 (todayDate prop)

fixes_applied.title: TaskItem — replaced Date allocations with string comparison using useToday()
fixes_applied.description: Replaced `new Date(task.dueDate) < new Date()` (2 Date objects per render per task) with `task.dueDate.split('T')[0] < today` using the shared useToday() hook. ISO date strings compare correctly as strings (lexicographic order matches chronological order for YYYY-MM-DD format). Zero Date allocations per TaskItem render for overdue checks.
fixes_applied.lines_changed: import line (added useToday), line 60 (useToday destructure), line 89-90 (string comparison)

new_files_created.description: Shared hook that returns a memoized { today: string, todayDate: Date } pair. Uses useState + useEffect with a setTimeout that fires at midnight to refresh the date key. The todayDate is derived via useMemo from the today string. This centralizes today-date computation and eliminates repeated new Date() + toDateKey() calls across components.
